name: Jan Hammer
description: Czech-born American musician, composer and record producer
abstract: Jan Hammer (Czech pronunciation: [ˈjan ˈɦamɛr]) (born 17 April 1948) is a Czech-American musician, composer, and record producer. He rose to prominence while playing keyboards with the Mahavishnu Orchestra during the early 1970s, as well as with his film scores for television and film including "Miami Vice Theme" and "Crockett's Theme", …

concerts.label: It's a Beautiful Day, Psychedelic/Folk Rock Band
Newport Jazz Festival: Cannonball Adderley Quintet, Oscar Peterson, and Mahavishnu Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: I had known Jan Hammer, the great Czech musician, because we used to share an apartment in Boston... But we shared an apartment together so I knew them just as friends and roommates.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Brecker
about_quotes.evidence: Miroslav and Mraz came to town shortly thereafter, as did Jan, we played together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Brecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 12, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: So I went out there. It was Gene Perla, Jan Hammer, and myself for a while.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: I would always be with one of them, sometimes with none of them, sometimes Jan Hammer. ... Merry-Go-Round, record date, real date, two pianos, Jan, Chick.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
